news.headline: Xi meets with international business executives Xinhua
Company seeks Trump support for quicker access to seabed metals, feared to harm oceans and boost manufacturing.
news.summary: Certain mining companies, alongside the Trump administration, are advocating for the extraction of metals to bolster manufacturing. However, concerns are arising from environmentalists and several nations regarding the potential detrimental impact of industrial mining on oceans. This debate emphasizes the importance of finding a balance between economic growth and environmental sustainability.

news.headline: TSMC to reduce semiconductor facility growth in Kumamoto, Japan, Nikkei
Crypto.com's CRO token surged 12% as SEC closes investigation into the firm - no action to be taken.
news.summary: Crypto.com's native token Cronos (CRYPTO: CRO) experienced a significant increase in value, becoming one of the top gainers on Thursday following news that the SEC concluded its investigation into the company without taking any enforcement action. Kris Marszalek, CEO of Crypto.com, confirmed the closure of the SEC investigation, stating, "The SEC's investigation into Crypto.com has concluded with no actions being pursued against us." The SEC did not provide an immediate response to Benzinga's inquiry. As a result of the announcement, the value of Cronos surged by 12%. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fake video of Trump unveiling Bitcoin Whitepaper in Oval Office circulating, but real crypto efforts remain serious.
news.summary: A fabricated video of President Trump presenting the Satoshi whitepaper has been circulating online, garnering attention. However, the genuine impact of Trump's involvement in the cryptocurrency realm should not be underestimated. His initiatives in this sector are gaining significant traction, with notable numbers of followers and supporters taking notice of his efforts. The fraudulent video may have caused a stir, but Trump's legitimate contributions to the crypto revolution deserve serious consideration.

news.headline: French state bank allocates $27M for local crypto projects, to receive tokens in return, supporting DeFi, staking & AI. Aim is to strengthen French blockchain ecosystem. Venture capitalists to invest $18B in crypto VC deals this year.
news.summary: France's state-owned bank, Bpifrance, announced plans to invest 25 million euros in cryptocurrencies supporting local projects. The bank will receive tokens in return for its investment in projects focused on DeFi, staking, tokenization, and AI. This initiative is backed by the French Ministry of Economy and Finance to bolster the French blockchain ecosystem. Bpifrance has been supporting the blockchain sector for a decade, having invested over 150 million euros and recently testing limited investments through tokens. The bank aims to increase French competitiveness in the digital assets field and stay ahead of the US in crypto strategy. Venture capitalists are increasingly participating in crypto deals paid in tokens, with 2025 projected to see crypto VC deals surpassing $18 billion. This move marks a significant increase from the $13.6 billion raised in 2024.

news.headline: Trump stops discussions on collective bargaining with federal unions, affecting thousands of workers.
DBV Tech secures funding of over $300M (€284.5M) for Viaskin® Peanut Patch to progress through FDA submission & US release.
2025 ZGC International Technology Trade Fair opens in Beijing with an eco-partnership conference, showcasing global tech innovations and trends.
news.summary: On March 27, 2025, the 2025 ZGC International Technology Trade Fair commenced in Beijing at the Zhongguancun Exhibition and Transfer Center. This event included the Opening Ceremony and Global Technology Commercialization Eco-Partnership Conference, focusing on the theme "New Quality." The fair aims to promote international collaboration in technology and innovation. Notable numbers include numerous exhibitors showcasing their products, with over 500 global technology companies participating. The event is expected to draw a significant attendance of over 10,000 professionals from various industries, fostering partnerships and advancements in the tech sector.

news.headline: JelloX study in Taiwan shows promise of 3D pathology over conventional techniques, to be validated in collaboration with Japanese institution.
news.summary: A research project conducted in Taiwan is anticipated to be confirmed through a new partnership with a prominent Japanese medical facility. The study focuses on comparing the enhanced sensitivity of 3D pathology to traditional sampling methods in various populations and healthcare environments. This collaboration signifies a critical advancement in medical research, aiming to improve diagnostic accuracy and effectiveness. The initiative, which commenced in Hsinchu on March 27, 2025, will leverage the expertise of both countries to analyze the potential benefits of 3D pathology in medical settings.

news.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin prices drop. Bitcoin at $87,151.49, Ethereum at $1,996.20, and Dogecoin at $0.1893. Market uncertainty due to Trump tariffs.
news.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin, saw minor decreases in their prices as President Trump's tariff announcements caused market uncertainties. Bitcoin dipped by 0.02% to $87,151.49, while Ethereum fell by 0.89% to $1,996.20, and Dogecoin decreased by 3.29% to $0.1893. Bitcoin's trading volume dropped by over 10% in 24 hours, with the cryptocurrency up 3.30% for the week but down almost 6.70% for the quarter. Ethereum also stagnated around $2,000 and fell by 39% in the first quarter. Long liquidations of over $113 million occurred in the last 24 hours, with bearish shorts dominating. Bitcoin's Open Interest saw a marginal rise, contrasting with Ethereum's futures funds, which dropped by 1.44%. The percentage of Bitcoin futures traders betting bearish exceeded those predicting price increases, indicating market uncertainty driven by macroeconomic factors.

news.headline: Delta Thailand becomes Asia's worst stock after a $30 billion loss.
CoreWeave's IPO prices at $40/share, raising $1.5B, lower than expected.
CoreWeave lowers IPO ambitions, now seeking $40/share instead of $47-$55 amid market volatility.
news.summary: The corporation initially projected its shares to be valued at $47 to $55, but is now seeking $40 per share due to the volatile stock market conditions.

news.headline: OpenAI implements rate limits in response to Ghibli-style AI art trend; GPUs overwhelmed. Revenue to triple to $12.7B this year.
news.summary: OpenAI has implemented rate limits on their ChatGPT model due to a viral trend of "Ghiblify" AI art which led to overwhelmed GPUs. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman participated in the trend, but the sudden increase in image requests strained their infrastructure. The upgraded ChatGPT-4o, launched on March 25, allowed users to create Studio Ghibli-style images, popularizing on social media. Altman mentioned the temporary rate limits to manage server load and ensure performance stability. OpenAI expected revenue to reach $12.7 billion this year, intending to release GPT-4.5 and GPT-5 soon. Notable figures like Elon Musk and David Sacks joined the trend. Overall, OpenAI imposed rate limits to maintain a smooth user experience amidst the influx of AI-generated art requests.

GIGABYTE releases B760 GEN5 motherboards with PCIe 5.0 support for AORUS ELITE, GIGABYTE GAMING, and UD series.
news.summary: In Taipei on March 27, 2025, GIGABYTE, a top computer brand, revealed the release of the latest B760 GEN5 series motherboards. This launch introduces PCIe 5.0 support to the popular AORUS ELITE, GIGABYTE GAMING, and UD series. These innovative motherboards aim to provide cutting-edge technology to an even wider range of consumers, advancing performance capabilities significantly.

news.headline: Xnito unveils NTA-8776 certified eBike helmet for kids featuring LED lights. World's first of its kind for young riders.
news.summary: A groundbreaking helmet has been introduced by Xnito in San Francisco on March 27, 2025, offering advanced protection for young riders on high-speed eBikes. This innovative helmet includes LED safety lights and features designed specifically for kids. Xnito, known for their expertise in eBike safety, has released the world's first eBike helmet for children, setting a new standard in safety gear for young riders.

news.headline: Taiwan investigates SMIC for recruiting Taiwanese high-tech talent
Hegseth embarks on crucial Asia tour amidst controversy over secretive messages on Yemen conflict.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the country's economy grew by 3.5% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations of a 2.8% increase. This marks the highest growth rate in the past five years. The increase was mainly attributed to a surge in consumer spending, which grew by 4.2% during the same period. Additionally, the manufacturing sector saw a 6% increase in output, contributing significantly to the overall growth of the economy. Analysts predict that if these growth trends continue, the country's GDP could exceed $2 trillion by the end of the year.

news.headline: UN Tourism and CAF launch Indigenous Tourism Challenge for Latin America and the Caribbean to support innovative projects showcasing indigenous culture and environment relationship.
news.summary: Madrid – The UN Tourism and CAF – Development Bank of Latin America and the Caribbean have jointly unveiled the Indigenous Tourism Challenge for Latin America and the Caribbean. The aim is to discover and back pioneering tourism ventures that showcase the cultural abundance and environmental connection of indigenous communities in the area. This initiative intends to empower these communities to preserve and rejuvenate their cultural heritage via genuine tourism experiences.

news.headline: CoreWeave, Inc. sets IPO pricing: 37.5M shares at $40 each, totaling $1.5B. AI Hyperscaler™ on track for public debut.
news.summary: The technology company CoreWeave, Inc. based in Livingston, N.J., disclosed the launch details of its initial public offering on March 27, 2025. The IPO comprises 37,500,000 shares of its Class A common stock priced at $40.00 per share for public offering. As an AI Hyperscaler™, CoreWeave focuses on advancing artificial intelligence technologies.

Gravity globally launches 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse' - a blend of text adventure, deck-building, and roguelike game Available on Google Play and App Store with seven DLCs.
news.summary: A new and innovative game that combines text-based adventure, deck-building, and roguelike elements is now available for purchase and download on both Google Play and the Apple App Store. Players can immerse themselves in captivating storylines that evolve based on their choices. The game, originating from SEOUL, South Korea, was released in March, with seven additional downloadable content packs introduced simultaneously with the base game.

Langer Heinrich Mine resumes operations after temporary suspension due to weather event. Paladin Energy Ltd provides update.
news.summary: In Perth, Australia on March 27, 2025, Paladin Energy Ltd (ASX:PDN, TSX:PDN, OTCQX:PALAF) announced the resumption of operations at the Langer Heinrich Mine (LHM) after a temporary suspension caused by severe weather. The company shared this update, indicating that production had restarted after the adverse event.

Shanton Pharma reports promising Phase 2b data on SAP-001 for uncontrolled gout patients.
news.summary: Shanton Pharma has revealed that SAP-001, their primary investigational compound, has significant potential as a groundbreaking treatment for uncontrolled gout. The company, based in Singapore and Princeton, N.J., shared this important update on March 27, 2025. SAP-001 is being developed as a first-of-its-kind solution in the gout treatment landscape. Shanton Pharma, a clinical-stage biotech firm, disclosed the key findings at the topline presentation.

news.headline: Prime Drink Group closed private placement, raising $99,960 through 100 Units, issuing 588,000 shares and warrants, total of $1,910,235.60.
news.summary: Prime Drink Group Corp. has successfully completed its non-brokered private placement, raising $99,960 through the issuance of 100 Units. Each Unit includes 588,000 common shares and 588,000 common share purchase warrants. The total gross proceeds for the entire Offering amounted to $1,910,235.60. This announcement came on March 27, 2025, in Montreal.

news.headline: SEC closes investigation into Crypto.com, no legal action taken. CEO claims they tried to hinder them, but they persevered. Recently, SEC dropped a series of crypto investigations, including Crypto.com. New SEC leadership adopts a friendlier approach. Crypto.com partners with Trump Media for "Made in America" ETFs.
news.summary: The US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has closed its investigation into Crypto.com with no action taken, marking the end of a seven-month-long probe. The investigation comes after the SEC issued a Wells notice in August, signaling possible legal action against the crypto platform. Crypto.com's CEO, Kris Marszalek, highlighted the challenges faced during the investigation but expressed gratitude for the community's support. The decision to close the investigation was welcomed by the firm's chief legal officer, Nick Lundgren. This move is part of a broader shift in the SEC's approach to crypto, with the current leadership under Mark Uyeda taking a friendlier stance compared to previous actions by former chair Gary Gensler. The SEC has dropped several crypto-related investigations and lawsuits in recent weeks, affecting companies like Coinbase, Consensys, and Robinhood. Additionally, Crypto.com's partnership with Trump Media to launch "Made in America" ETFs later this year was announced. The exchange will provide infrastructure and custody services for the ETFs, which may include tokens like Bitcoin, Ether, Solana, XRP, and Cronos. The article raises key questions regarding the SEC's evolving stance on crypto regulation and enforcement.

Southwest Gas sees rise in 811 requests for marking underground lines, leading to fewer damages; celebrating National Safe Digging Month in April.

The data shows a substantial boost in requests to 811 for marking underground lines, almost reaching a remarkable number. This development is particularly timely as April marks National Safe Digging Month.

Medi-Ops and Southwest Transplant Alliance complete first commercial on-board courier flight with Paragonix KidneyVault, expanding kidney transplant access. #milestone
news.summary: Cutting-edge partnership allows for the transportation of organs over large distances, increasing opportunities for crucial kidney transplants. Recently announced on March 27, 2025, the collaboration between Medi-Ops, a medical logistics group harnessing technology, and Southwest Transplant Alliance (STA) aims to enhance access to life-saving procedures. This development in the medical field comes as a significant advancement, with the potential to positively impact a vast number of patients in need of kidney transplants.
